The result includes several narrower library/tool terms that do not appear in the proposed title or description, such as "pandas", "numpy", "scikit-learn", "matplotlib", "seaborn", "jupyter", and "jupyter notebook". These reduce query quality and look like padded candidate terms, though they do not break the required recall behavior. There is also some minor redundancy between "python data science" and "python for data science".

The query list would be stronger if it removed unmentioned library/tool-only terms and added a small number of recall-oriented variants such as "py data science" or "data analytics with python" while keeping the focus on Python and data science/data analysis.
